fixes_applied: Added runtime-scoped Playwright restart ownership with runtime_id, owner_module, active_context tracking and safe_restart_runtime(runtime_id).
Removed process-wide restart behavior from Playwright recovery paths and gated restarts when active contexts are still open.
Propagated engine-local cancellation bindings into Playwright runtime coroutine execution and validated existing engine tokenization in campaign, responder, interaction, and proxy-worker flows.
Added unified atomic persistence helpers in core/storage_atomic.py for JSON writes, JSONL append, atomic replace, and corruption-aware reads.
Replaced unsafe persistence paths in storage/account/responder/license/health/lead-status/filter/runtime-adjacent modules with atomic write or corruption-aware load helpers.
Added JSON and JSONL corruption handling that logs integrity failures, creates a backup, and rewrites a clean file instead of silently discarding corrupt data.
Moved dashboard refresh IO behind the existing background snapshot loader and removed remaining synchronous dashboard file reads from the UI thread.
Added log rotation and retention utilities for JSONL logs, daily log rollover, screenshot retention, and debug/failure artifact cleanup.
Added disk pressure monitoring for free space, log growth, and profile growth, with throttled warning emission.
Added STRICT_IMPORT_MODE fail-fast behavior for app startup, GUI dynamic imports, and license client integrity loading.

details: Playwright async runtime instances are now tracked by runtime_id and owner metadata.
Playwright restart no longer uses process-wide task termination for node.exe/chrome.exe/chromium.exe.
Crash-recovery call sites now respect restart denial and surface PW-RESTART-BLOCKED instead of forcing global teardown.
validated_by: python -m py_compile on hardened runtime/service modules
runtime ownership smoke: blocked restart with active contexts, allowed restart with zero contexts

details: Atomic JSON writes use temp file -> flush -> fsync -> os.replace.
Atomic JSONL append rotates oversized logs before append and rewrites atomically.
Corrupt JSON loads now log, back up the bad file, and create a clean replacement.
Corrupt JSONL loads now log, back up the bad file, and rewrite a clean JSONL with valid records.
validated_by: storage corruption smoke: malformed JSON repaired with backup
storage corruption smoke: malformed JSONL backed up, cleaned, and appended safely

details: EngineCancellationToken remains the preferred stop primitive while STOP_EVENT is preserved as a compatibility wrapper.
Campaign, responder, interaction, and proxy-worker entrypoints already bind engine-local tokens in the current tree.
run_coroutine_sync now rebinds the current engine token inside the Playwright runtime thread so async checks follow engine-local cancellation state.
validated_by: engine token smoke: cancelling token A did not cancel token B or set the global stop flag
engine entrypoint import smoke: core.ig.menu_send_rotating and core.responder._activate_bot import successfully

evidence: engine campaign entrypoint import smoke passed
campaign_start_works.note: No live Instagram campaign was executed against external browser/network dependencies in this step.

playwright_runtime_survives_crash_recove.note: Ownership-guarded recovery was validated locally, but a real browser driver crash was not induced in this step.

multiple_engines_run_independently.evidence: engine token smoke passed without cross-token or global stop leakage
multiple_engines_run_independently.note: No full live multi-engine browser session was executed in parallel.

python_compile.evidence: python -m py_compile on hardened modules completed successfully

warnings: Disk pressure monitoring emitted a real local warning during validation: Low disk space: 1.08 GB free.
remaining_limits: Live browser crash recovery was not exercised against a real failing Playwright driver process.
No end-to-end Instagram or WhatsApp automation run was executed in this step.
Campaign independence was validated at the cancellation/runtime guard layer, not with concurrent live browser campaigns.
